title: deschampsia cespitosa subsp. borealis
intro: Deschampsia cespitosa subsp. borealis, also known as northern hair grass, is a subspecies of grass native to northern Europe and Asia, characterized by its delicate, feathery panicles and slender stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, such as cramps and bloating. Consuming northern hair grass is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and providing antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, although its uses and potential effects should be further researched and confirmed by medical professionals.
